body:
Closes #214
Closes #9
Closes #46
Closes #63
Closes #411

## Preview
https://deploy-preview-468--base-ui.netlify.app/base-ui/react-menu/

## To do
- [x] #411 
- [x] #46 
- [x] #63 
- [x] #9 
- [X] Nested menus
- [x] Animations
- [x] Docs
- [x] Configure <kbd>Esc</kbd> key behavior (close all/close one submenu level)
- [x] `keepMounted` prop
- [x] Configurable delay and open on hover

### In separate PRs
- [x] #52 
- [x] #53 
- [x] #70

comments.body:
## Netlify deploy preview https://deploy-preview-468--base-ui.netlify.app/

Generated by :no_entry_sign: dangerJS against c66dcb1f87335bfc8407e014a0ac6f4f0f76aaed

@michaldudak 

- Submenu shouldn't close when the pointer leaves its popup. It should close when focus moves to a menuitem that is not the submenu's trigger, and that is not disabled.
- When you open a submenu via pointer, focus should remain on its trigger. Pressing right should move focus into the submenu.
- 750ms typeahead threshold is too long.
- When opening via space/enter, it should trigger by keydown rather than keypress.
- Vid attached of placing pointer over a menuitem to focus it, then pressing up, and two menuitems are highlighted. Only one menuitem should ever be focused.
- Vid attached of some crazy ass bug

https://github.com/mui/base-ui/assets/805073/044b66aa-fa08-429c-87d8-51d18ee04f56
https://github.com/mui/base-ui/assets/805073/294742ba-0bcf-4034-abd8-5b5f318eac67

@colmtuite 

Fixed:
- submenus don't close on mouseout anymore
- focus stays on the highlighted submenu trigger after it opens (EDIT: there's still something weird going on on first interaction; I'm investigating it)
- changed typeahead threshold to 350ms
- bugs you recorded are fixed
@michaldudak When you close the menu via ESC, focus is not returned to the trigger.
@michaldudak Clicking the trigger repeatedly should toggle the menu open/closed

Is the space/enter key opening broken? I remember it worked last week (coming here from the monthly meeting link 😅)


https://github.com/mui/base-ui/assets/16889233/06682b75-3472-4b5b-a71d-3935129bfc9e
It is for now, but bear in mind it's still a draft and I'm working on it :)

When you move the cursor very quickly across menu items, it seems like focus is not able to keep up.
https://github.com/mui/base-ui/assets/805073/7f1f33cb-172f-47aa-9519-bc8f76493625

> Clicking the trigger repeatedly should toggle the menu open/closed

In MacOS, the native menus open on the trigger's mousedown but close on mouseup. WDYT about adopting this behavior?

MacOS has some sort of interval. If you click, hold for 50ms, then release over the trigger, the menu remains open. If you click, hold for 500ms, then release over the trigger, the menu closes.

After chatting, we decided this might have two issues:

1. It may be problematic for some older or disabled users who can't confidently click the button fast enough.
2. There will be some perf hit to set a timer on each click.

So we decided to close the menu when you click, hold, drag, and release outside of the trigger.

Some bugs:

1. The focus doesn't synchronize once pointer-events stop being blocked and the cursor is at rest

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/68f68436-15bb-48d9-a52a-4ba61896eb37

2. The submenu is left open when quickly navigating with ArrowLeft/ArrowRight even though focus is on the parent trigger

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9fe69fcc-f3e4-4353-9e05-aa11860c56f3

> The focus doesn't synchronize once pointer-events stop being blocked and the cursor is at rest

This looks like a bug in Floating UI. The same behavior can be seen in https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/admiring-lamport-5wt3yg.

I can take a look at it, unless you have an idea how to solve it easily.

> The submenu is left open when quickly navigating with ArrowLeft/ArrowRight even though focus is on the parent trigger

This one's interesting. I was only able to reproduce it when animations are enabled, and it seems like the first element of the submenu isn't focused when the menu is open. I'm still investigating the root cause.

> This looks like a bug in Floating UI. The same behavior can be seen in https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/admiring-lamport-5wt3yg.

I don't see the bug in that sandbox? When it rests the focus synchronizes. (I think it may be manually handled externally in the components, not the internal hooks though)

Actually I see it doesn't synchronize unless the element underneath the cursor is a submenu trigger. I think that was intentional since nothing opens on `hover` and doesn't need to/shouldn't synchronize, but it's been a while since I built that.
What do you think about not disabling pointer events in these cases? This will also help in cases where someone wants to select a menu item below a submenu trigger right after the submenu opens.

- [Do not block pointer-events in safe polygon](https://github.com/mui/base-ui/pull/468/commits/15ffd83a65c771becd3e9d8ff8b0502d1da04d1c) - why was this added?

> This will also help in cases where someone wants to select a menu item below a submenu trigger right after the submenu opens.

The `requireIntent` feature of `safePolygon` handles this scenario. It prevents them from being too aggressively blocked by using a timeout

- If another button is focused instead of the parent menu trigger, and a submenu item is clicked, it returns focus to that instead of the parent. The top-level menu items aren't affected. 
- `escapeClosesParents` as a prop name sounds slightly awkward

> [Do not block pointer-events in safe polygon](https://github.com/mui/base-ui/pull/468/commits/15ffd83a65c771becd3e9d8ff8b0502d1da04d1c) - why was this added?

This is a workaround for "The focus doesn't synchronize once pointer-events stop being blocked and the cursor is at rest".

> If another button is focused instead of the parent menu trigger, and a submenu item is clicked, it returns focus to that instead of the parent. The top-level menu items aren't affected.

Could you record a video showing this issue?

> Could you record a video showing this issue?

Bug seems to be gone in the latest deploy

> This is a workaround for "The focus doesn't synchronize once pointer-events stop being blocked and the cursor is at rest".

It only needs to synchronize focus for a submenu trigger, not a menu item. This is because the submenu item opens a menu on hover, so it needs to focus style applied. The Floating UI menu demo handles only that
@atomiks I restored `blockPointerEvents` and managed to solve the issue with CSS.

Bug when traversing back to the submenu trigger, menu doesn't open

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/162b1b95-74bb-4a82-9fc1-927a6b835d00

@michaldudak Feedback based on our chat:

1. Add the Arrow component
2. Consider making focus looping configurable. I vote it should be.
3. Rename `positionStrategy` to `positionMethod` across all components.
@colmtuite I did 1 and 2. `positionStrategy` rename should be done on the other components first in a separate PR.

@michaldudak In this vid, the menu is initially opening above the trigger, then quickly repositioning itself below.
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dee98c5c-a19b-47ab-8f8b-2c2f07b0eb30

labels.name: component: menu
labels.description: Changes related to the menu component.

labels.name: breaking change
labels.description: Introduces changes that are not backward compatible.

title: [menu] Overhaul the component API
